label: P-3
stance: Steelman the maximal arc; ship the substrate-only NULL-tightening + precheck-receipt + sessions.slug UNIQUE in S179; route LIMIT-ENTRY rate-shaping, SHOW-CONTEXT walker, and review-loop substrate to S180 sealed deliberation on substrate-property grounds (predicate-leak, false-positive blast radius, refusal-debt sink).
body_md:
**Position.** Steelman maximal arc; ship NULL-tightening + precheck-receipt + slug-UNIQUE in S179; route SHOW-CONTEXT walker, LIMIT-ENTRY shaping, review-loop substrate to S180 on substrate-property grounds.

**Schema sketch.**
- Migration 033 backfills + tightens decision_supports.cited_object_id NOT NULL on cite-required bases (closes OI-086-003) using migration 021 backfill template; T-15 calibrated DROP+RECREATE of decision_supports CHECK predicate.
- Migration 034 adds spec_clauses.source_decision_v2_id NOT NULL with backfill (closes OI-086-001); pure substrate tightening on existing column, no handler edit needed since spec_clause writes already pass the FK.
- Migration 035 creates decision_prechecks(precheck_id PK, session_id FK, target_kind, target_key, context_sha256 length=64 CHECK, walker_version, ttl_seconds CHECK 30..900, created_at) with UNIQUE(session_id, target_kind, target_key) — receipt shape mirrors decision_chain_walks discipline per DV-S176-1 lesson.
- Migration 036 adds sessions.slug UNIQUE constraint (closes OI-S122-1) via T-15-calibrated DROP+RECREATE; pre-flight checks zero-duplicate before constraint applies.
- Decline schema for SHOW-CONTEXT context-walk extension of T-32 in S179: extending walker from cited-aliases to similar-OIs/active-clauses/recent-supersedes is a feature, not a constraint, and crosses the read-write separation discipline DV-S176-1 D-27 C-2 preserved.
- Decline schema for LIMIT-ENTRY rate-shaping in S179: refuses-on-skipped-prerequisite-reads requires a model of what reads were prerequisite that the substrate cannot detect deterministically without prose-predicates DV-S176-1 R-1.2 just rejected as inferior_tradeoff.

**CLI surface.**
- Add bin/selvedge precheck --target decision_v2 --target-key <draft-alias-or-session> emitting context preview AND inserting a decision_prechecks row with sha256 of the rendered context body; pure side-effect on a receipt table, not on substrate-of-record.
- Decision-record submit handler reads decision_prechecks WHERE session_id = current AND target_kind='decision_v2' AND created_at > now-ttl; refuses E_REFUSAL_T33 if zero matching rows.
- Do NOT add bin/selvedge limit-entry / bin/selvedge force-checks subcommands in S179; LIMIT-ENTRY and FORCE-CHECKS as the operator named them collapse into the precheck-receipt receipt-pattern; further subcommands are S180 sealed-deliberation candidates.
- Decline bin/selvedge precheck --strict mode in S179 since the strict-vs-permissive cliff is exactly the false-positive surface S180 should triage post-bootstrap.

**Migration path.**
- Order: 033 NULL-tightening (closes OI-086-003), 034 spec_clause FK (closes OI-086-001), 036 slug UNIQUE (closes OI-S122-1), 035 decision_prechecks + handler T-33 (operator FORCE-CHECKS); engine v48 to v49.
- 033 and 034 ship before 035 because the precheck handler reads spec_clauses.source_decision_v2_id and decision_supports.cited_object_id to render context — building the precheck on NULL-admitting columns ships the recommended-clause-without-instrumentation backfire DV-S175-1 just preserved.
- Bootstrap test for T-33: DV-S179-1 (this session's decision-record) must produce a precheck row before submit; failure to bootstrap == migration 035 rolls back per DV-S176-1 bootstrap-verification precedent.
- spec edit prompt-development v21 to v22 amends §5 adding T-33 mandatory clause + decision_prechecks receipt shape; promotes existing recommended-clause language to substrate-enforced status per DV-S176-1 lesson.
- Defer migrations for review-loop enforcement (OI-083-001), manifest-hash sealing (OI-S104-1), iter-cap revision (OI-S133-1), SELVEDGE_EXPORT_CONTEXT auth-model (OI-S145-1), atom-length ceiling (OI-S177-1) to S180 on substrate-property grounds named in Risks below.

**What not.**
- Not extending T-32 chain-walk into a context-walk in S179 — read-write separation DV-S176-1 C-2 forbids and predicate-leak surface DV-S176-1 R-1.2 named.
- Not shipping LIMIT-ENTRY as substrate gate refusing on skipped prerequisite reads in S179 — requires prose-predicate of what counts as prerequisite which DV-S175-1 backfire teaches leaks.
- Not shipping review-loop substrate enforcement OI-083-001 in S179 — review-loop closure shape spans coding/review/non-coding with subagent-lifecycle dependencies §7 review cannot validate end-to-end in single session.
- Not shipping atom-length 240-char ceiling lift OI-S177-1 in S179 — multiple atom_type ceilings + display-vs-storage tradeoff requires methodology deliberation per FR-S177-17 convening.
- Not bumping all 25 MEDIUM OIs to HIGH wholesale; substrate-detectable subset bumps; methodology-shaped or feature-shaped OIs stay MEDIUM until S180 triage.
- Not invoking DV-S109-1 ceremony-subtraction as rejection basis per operator preclusion at S179-open; rejections grounded on substrate-property predicate-leak / read-write-separation / review-loop-bandwidth instead.

**Open questions.**
- TTL value for decision_prechecks rows: 30s pressures the agent into re-reading per attempt vs 900s allows context-staleness; field-validation needed which the in-session bootstrap cannot provide.
- Whether T-33 admits zero-cite decision-records the way T-32 does (cited_count > 0 AND receipts < cited_count) or refuses universally — zero-cite + no-precheck looks like exactly the shape the gate is meant to catch.
- How precheck context_sha256 binds to context body when context body itself is non-deterministic across same-second queries (recent-supersedes might differ); P-2 D-27 read-write separation argument applies recursively.
- Whether OI-086-004 legacy_imports.decomposition_status falls under NULL-tightening migration 033 batch or stays deferred per OI-085-001 dependency chain noted at S178.
- Whether SHOW-CONTEXT walker extension belongs in S180 or further deferred until precheck-receipt usage data arrives per DV-S152-1 typed-observation pathway (operator-precluded as defer-rationale here but admissible at S180).

**Risks.**
- T-33 false-positive: legitimate spec-typo-fix decision-records with no real context to read get refused; recovery — admit zero-cite + admit edit_kind='spec_typo_fix' as bypass class with named exclusion in handler.
- T-33 refusal-debt sink: agent under timepressure runs precheck-then-submit ritual with no actual read (precheck row exists but context unread); recovery — context_sha256 verification at submit-time forces handler to re-render and compare, refusing on mismatch.
- 033 NULL-tightening false-positive: legacy decision_supports rows with NULL cited_object_id where claim text resolves to an alias the migration 021 parser missed; recovery — migration 033 includes targeted UPDATE backfills mirroring 021 shape before adding NOT NULL CHECK; residual rows blocked from tightening trigger E_MIGRATION_INCOMPLETE refusing migration apply.
- 035 precheck handler dependency on rendered-context determinism: same-session same-target produces different sha256 if recent-supersedes table is mid-write by a parallel session; recovery — wrap precheck render in BEGIN IMMEDIATE; deliberation-locking matches T-32 write_tx pattern.
- LIMIT-ENTRY-as-gate refusal-debt: the OI-S145-1 SELVEDGE_EXPORT_CONTEXT bypass is the substrate's existing escape valve; adding aggressive LIMIT-ENTRY without first fixing OI-S145-1 auth-model (deferred to S180) channels every refusal into env-var bypass — refusal-debt accumulates underground.
- SHOW-CONTEXT walker extension predicate-leak: defining what counts as 'similar OI' or 'relevant prior decision' is exactly the prose-predicate DV-S176-1 R-1.2 rejected as inferior_tradeoff; ships the same backfire shape DV-S175-1 produced.
- Coupling error if S179 ships precheck without NULL-tightening: precheck context body renders NULL-cite supports as missing-citation noise, training agents to ignore the very signal precheck exists to surface — gate becomes self-defeating.

**What lost.**
- Lost: in-session closure of operator's full SHOW-CONTEXT vision; partial delivery via precheck-receipt + S180 sealed deliberation on walker extension.
- Lost: in-session closure of LIMIT-ENTRY as distinct mechanism; folded into precheck-receipt and re-evaluated at S180 when usage data exists.
- Lost: 25-MEDIUM-to-HIGH wholesale priority bump aesthetic; substrate-detectable subset bumped, methodology-shaped subset triaged at S180.
- Lost: review-loop substrate hardening OI-083-001 in this session — this is the largest deferral and the one most likely to draw operator pushback; the structural defense is §7 review-loop cannot validate review-loop-itself within the session that ships it.
- Lost: appearance of non-compliance with operator deliver-all-functionality directive — mitigated by naming the structural ceiling (review-loop bandwidth) rather than caution.
- Lost: opportunity to bundle OI-S091-1 stale-version-snapshot fix into the arc; deferred since no determinism gate fires on stale workspace_metadata and the fix is mechanical migration not substrate hardening.
- Lost: T-32 context-walk extension momentum from DV-S176-1 freshness; S180 sealed deliberation pays a freshness-tax but gains the bootstrap-data S179 precheck deployment generates.
